reference_scope: amule-official; emule-official-0.50a; mldonkey-official; wireshark-official; protocol-docs
current_surface: Historical tracker mentions older deleted references.
target_or_prune_reason: Use only the current authoritative local references. Treat the historical ED2K tracker as background, not authority. The RA2 reference map in overview.md records the concrete authoritative entry points.
user_impact: Future ED2K work stops depending on stale reference assumptions.

reference_scope: aMule/eMule/MLDonkey session and socket ownership
current_surface: aria2-next RequestGroup, DownloadEngine, Command/EventPoll, Ed2kAttribute, Ed2kCommand, Ed2kKadCommand
target_or_prune_reason: Keep aria2-next lifecycle ownership. Current source inventory is mapped in overview.md. Ed2kCommand remains the overloaded TCP owner for server and peer sessions. Ed2kKadCommand remains the single UDP owner for both ED2K server UDP and Kad. Split only when a later checkpoint proves a correctness, capability-truth, or maintainability need.
user_impact: No sidecar runtime or second event loop is introduced.

reference_scope: aMule ED2KLink; eMule ED2KLink; protocol-docs link grammar
target_or_prune_reason: Aligned file, server, serverlist, nodeslist, and search link parsing. File links preserve MD4 root hashes, part hashes, AICH roots, inline sources, source hashes, and crypt options. Path separators in file names are normalized before aria2-next output path ownership. File size validation follows the 2^38 byte eMule/aMule limit. Search links are parsed as metadata and not treated as normal downloads.
user_impact: Users can paste common ED2K links without unsafe filenames or lost metadata.

reference_scope: aMule/eMule ServerList and Server; MLDonkey server state; Wireshark tags
current_surface: ed2k_server.*; Ed2kAttribute serverStates; session hidden state
target_or_prune_reason: server.met parsing accepts current headers, preserves name, description, dynip, users, files, max users, soft/hard file limits, UDP flags, LowID users, UDP key, and TCP/UDP obfuscation ports, then merges them into aria2-next ServerState without overwriting existing nonzero state with absent tags.
user_impact: Server discovery state survives restart and carries useful TCP/UDP capability metadata.

reference_scope: aMule/eMule Kad contacts; MLDonkey nodes.dat; protocol docs
current_surface: ed2k_kad.*; Ed2kKadState; session hidden Kad state
target_or_prune_reason: nodes.dat parsing supports count-first, versioned, and bootstrap edition formats. It filters empty endpoints, zero UDP ports, Kad1-only contacts, and old UDP port 53 contacts. Versioned files with no verified contacts mark retained contacts verified for bootstrap, matching aMule/eMule behavior. Durable Kad state stays in aria2-next hidden session state.
user_impact: Kad bootstrap remains native and restart-safe without preserving obsolete Kad1 contacts.

reference_scope: eMule/aMule part.met; known.met; known2.met; clients.met; MLDonkey state
current_surface: aria2-next control files, session serializer, SharedStore, UploadQueue
target_or_prune_reason: Legacy ED2K databases are replaced by aria2-next control files, hidden session records, SharedStore metadata, and UploadQueue peer-credit state. Import of part.met, known.met, known2.met, or clients.met is pruned until a concrete migration requirement is approved.
user_impact: No second ED2K database stack is introduced.

reference_scope: aMule/eMule ServerSocket; MLDonkey server protocol; Wireshark ED2K dissector; protocol docs
target_or_prune_reason: TCP server handling keeps reference-compatible hash+size GetSources payloads, tolerates IDChange extension tails, preserves nonzero UDP/limit metadata across TCP status updates, parses TCP status separately from UDP status, accepts server ident and server-list trailing data, and treats malformed FoundSources/search result packets as request failures instead of server failures.
user_impact: Server source discovery handles modern and older server formats without clearing useful persisted metadata.

reference_scope: aMule/eMule ServerUDPSocket; MLDonkey UDP behavior; Wireshark ED2K UDP
current_surface: Ed2kKadCommand ED2K UDP path; ed2k_server.*; ed2k_peer.*
target_or_prune_reason: ED2K server UDP stays on the existing UDP command path. Status replies accept reference extensions, clear matched challenges, and update UDP capability metadata. Global source requests use hash-only legacy payloads or hash+size GETSOURCES2 forms. Packed source replies preserve valid sources when later packets are for other hashes or contain bogus tail bytes.
user_impact: UDP improves discovery without a second subsystem.

reference_scope: protocol-docs callback; aMule/eMule callback handling; MLDonkey indirect addresses
target_or_prune_reason: Server-mediated LowID callback remains the supported callback path. HighID sources are scheduled directly, LowID sources require a server callback request before direct scheduling, callback failures mark the matching LowID peer impossible, and required-crypt callback endpoints are skipped until crypt transport is implemented. Buddy-only, direct UDP callback, and GUI-only callback ownership are pruned for now because no non-pruned transfer path depends on them.
user_impact: LowID behavior is deterministic and avoids unsupported callback claims.

reference_scope: aMule/eMule ClientTCPSocket and ListenSocket; Wireshark eMule dissector
target_or_prune_reason: Hello and HelloAnswer carry the reference misc-options capability tags. Standalone eMule info keeps old-style feature tags for legacy compatibility. Local peer-info construction is shared between active download and shared-only peer commands.
user_impact: Peers see accurate client identity and compatible handshake packets.

reference_scope: aMule/eMule misc options; MLDonkey defaults; Wireshark tags
target_or_prune_reason: Local capability claims advertise implemented AICH, Unicode, compression, SX1, SX2, and large-file support. Multipacket, extended multipacket, secure-ident, crypt, Kad, and file identifiers remain unadvertised until their send, receive, state, error, and fallback paths are implemented or safely handled.
user_impact: Modern peers do not select packet flows aria2-next cannot complete.

reference_scope: aMule/eMule encrypted sockets and obfuscation metadata; Wireshark crypt fields
current_surface: Source crypt metadata exists; no complete encrypted transport owner is evident
target_or_prune_reason: Parse and preserve source/server crypt metadata where existing source and server paths carry it. Keep crypt transport unadvertised and skip require-crypt sources until an existing socket owner implements the encrypted transport path.
user_impact: Avoids failed encrypted paths caused by false capability claims.

reference_scope: eMule secure identification; aMule credits; Wireshark SEC_IDENT/PUBLIC_KEY/SIGNATURE
current_surface: Upload credit counters exist; secure-ident flow is incomplete
target_or_prune_reason: Keep secure identification unadvertised for now. Credit counters may persist, but identity verification is not claimed until a full public-key and signature packet flow is implemented.
user_impact: Upload credits stay honest without claiming identity verification.

reference_scope: aMule/eMule DownloadClient and ClientTCPSocket; protocol docs; Wireshark multipacket
target_or_prune_reason: Use the reference plain fallback flow because local multipacket and file-identifier capability bits are not advertised. Current peer sessions send and receive OP_REQUESTFILENAME, OP_REQFILENAMEANSWER, OP_SETREQFILEID, OP_FILESTATUS, OP_HASHSETREQUEST, OP_HASHSETANSWER, OP_STARTUPLOADREQ, OP_ACCEPTUPLOADREQ, OP_QUEUERANK/OP_QUEUERANKING, OP_FILEREQANSNOFIL, OP_REQUESTSOURCES/2, OP_AICHFILEHASHREQ/ANS, and shared-file responses through native aria2-next state. Deprecated multipacket and file-identifier packet families stay disabled until their complete state surface exists.
user_impact: Peer sessions can reach queue or transfer state without triggering unimplemented modern envelopes.

reference_scope: eMule FileIdentifier; aMule KnownFile; protocol docs
target_or_prune_reason: Represent file identity through the implemented aria2-next metadata model: ED2K root hash, file size, optional MD4 part hashset, optional AICH root, and existing compatible comparison rules. The eMule FileIdentifier packet envelope is not advertised and is deferred because the plain hashset and AICH paths already carry the implemented identity data without adding a second identifier protocol surface.
user_impact: Large-file and AICH-aware peers get truthful identity data on supported paths, while unsupported FileIdentifier envelopes are not claimed.

reference_scope: aMule/eMule transfer states; protocol docs
target_or_prune_reason: Use aria2-next PeerState, SegmentMan, and Ed2kPeerTransfer for the reference transfer-state behavior. 32-bit and I64 part requests are generated with protocol-width bounds, incoming normal/compressed parts validate hash, range, and data length before disk writes, duplicate completed data is ignored, no-file/out-of-parts/cancel/disconnect/bad-packet/corrupt-piece paths clear requested ranges, and failed peers enter retry/backoff without halting unrelated sources.
user_impact: Failed peers recover cleanly and valid peers can transfer safely through existing aria2-next scheduling.

reference_scope: aMule/eMule PartFile/KnownFile/AICH; protocol-docs corruption handling
target_or_prune_reason: Normal, I64, and compressed part data continue through existing range validation and disk paths. MD4 hashset request and validation now use the ED2K theoretical part-hash count: files below PARTSIZE have no hashset entries, exact PARTSIZE multiples include the trailing empty MD4 hash, and root hash validation follows the reference hashset model. AICH remains the implemented recovery-assistance path while MD4 remains authoritative for final integrity.
user_impact: Hashset exchange and shared-file responses no longer reject valid exact-PARTSIZE files or advertise invalid small-file hashsets.

reference_scope: aMule/eMule source management and DeadSourceList; MLDonkey source handling
target_or_prune_reason: Source priority, server/OBFU/inline/resume/SX/Kad source flags, callback constraints, queue rank, availability, dead-source backoff, duplicate rejection, requested-range cleanup, idle segment reclaim, and endgame behavior stay in native PeerState, SegmentMan, and policy ownership. Require-crypt endpoints are preserved as metadata but skipped by connect scheduling until encrypted transport is implemented.
user_impact: Downloads avoid tight loops, unsupported encrypted paths, and unsafe duplicate writes.

reference_scope: aMule/eMule Source Exchange; Wireshark source packets
target_or_prune_reason: SX1, SX2, and SX2 v4 packets preserve user hash, server endpoint, crypt options, deduplication, loopback/self filtering, and existing peer backoff state. Source Exchange request opcode selection now follows remote capability truth: SX2 uses OP_REQUESTSOURCES2 with version/options/hash, SX1 version greater than 1 uses hash-only OP_REQUESTSOURCES, and unsupported peers receive no SX request. Direct callback metadata is limited to preserved crypt/source metadata until a later Kad or encrypted-transport owner needs more.
user_impact: Peers can discover extra sources safely without triggering unsupported Source Exchange or crypt flows.

reference_scope: aMule/eMule Kademlia; MLDonkey Kad; protocol docs; Kademlia paper
target_or_prune_reason: Align classic Kad nodes.dat, bootstrap, hello, routing, traversal, source search, source publish, keyword search, firewalled checks, refresh, and durable state. Prune obsolete Kad1/UI behavior.
user_impact: Kad discovery remains native and maintainable.

reference_scope: aMule/eMule SharedFileList/KnownFile; MLDonkey share model
target_or_prune_reason: Align completed-file sharing, imported file hashing, known-file metadata, duplicate handling, file status, hashset, AICH, Source Exchange answer, and part serving.
user_impact: aria2-next can behave as a cooperative file source where enabled.

reference_scope: aMule/eMule UploadQueue/UploadClient/ClientCredits; protocol docs
target_or_prune_reason: Align queue admission, queue rank, slot limits, uploaded/downloaded counters, waiting state, UDP reask rank, and credit behavior. Prune friend slots and UI-only controls.
user_impact: Peers see valid upload queue behavior without legacy UI policy.

reference_scope: aMule/eMule SearchList/SearchExpr; MLDonkey search; protocol docs
target_or_prune_reason: Align server and Kad search, expression terms, typed tags, result merge, source counts, complete source counts, file type, extension, media metadata, source networks, more-results, and generated ED2K links.
user_impact: Motrix Next can search and start normal ED2K tasks.

reference_scope: aria2-next RPC patterns; Motrix integration needs; legacy DTOs only as contrast
target_or_prune_reason: Expose stable ED2K task/search/share/upload/Kad/server/peer fields only when they reflect implemented behavior.
user_impact: Integrators receive accurate status without unsupported claims.

reference_scope: aMule GUI/web/text client; eMule Windows UI; MLDonkey UI surfaces
target_or_prune_reason: Prune GUI-only, daemon-shell, web UI, telnet UI, remote GUI, chat, preview, collection, captcha, rating, and shared-directory browsing surfaces.
user_impact: No legacy UI compatibility is promised.

reference_scope: aMule/eMule UPnP/NAT helpers; MLDonkey port helpers
current_surface: aria2-next listen-port options and existing networking
target_or_prune_reason: Replace legacy UPnP/NAT-PMP/router-test stacks with explicit aria2-next listen-port configuration unless a native owner is later approved.
user_impact: No extra NAT dependency is introduced.
